refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle by optimizing load handling, travel efficiency, and dump operations.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Decomposing it into sub-FRs targeting each phase of the cycle (load, travel, dump) allows focused design parameters and clearer verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time components separately and verify that their sum meets the p95 ≤ CT_target under site conditions.

candidate_dps: Automated load handling system with optimized grip and lift
Optimized travel path planning and speed control
Efficient dump mechanism with rapid release and reset
selected_dp.selected_dp: Automated load handling system with optimized grip and lift; Optimized travel path planning and speed control; Efficient dump mechanism with rapid release and reset

potential_sources: Load handling affects travel start time
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces to minimize cross-dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate site condition data for travel optimization
Risk of timing mismatch if modules are not well synchronized
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit synchronization DP or interface to manage timing dependencies.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components responsible for load handling, travel, and dump operations

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send cycle time and operation status data to NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive operation commands from NGHE Solution or Teleoperation

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle contains the physical mechanisms for load, travel, and dump; low diffusion risk.
NGHE Solution manages high-level coordination but does not contain physical DP.
Teleoperation is external control, not responsible for physical DP.
Safety Services unrelated to cycle time DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Physical DP for cycle time optimization is best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with NGHE Solution for tasking and telemetry
Risk of tight coupling if Vehicle module is not well modularized internally
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and consider sub-module decomposition within Vehicle for load, travel, and dump.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is clear and sub-FRs map logically to selected DPs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if Vehicle module interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module is appropriate containment boundary for physical DP; external interfaces are well identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle times and telemetry data from Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical DP to Vehicle; high-level coordination remains in NGHE Solution.

recommended_revisions: Add synchronization DP/interface to manage timing dependencies between load, travel, and dump.
Refine Vehicle internal modularization to reduce coupling.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments are consistent, verifiable, and architecturally sound. No immediate need to zigzag or revise.

revise.why: No critical issues identified requiring immediate revision.

assumptions: Site conditions and duty cycle data are accurate and available.
Vehicle module can be internally modularized to handle sub-functions.
open_risks: Timing synchronization between load, travel, and dump operations.
Interface complexity between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Cycle time measurements under representative duty cycles.
Interface definition documents between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
